title: Richly Roasted Egyptian Duck Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Egyptian dish featuring roasted duck in a nimono-style simmer, perfect for special occasions and low-carb diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the duck breast with cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the duck breast until browned on all sides, then remove from the pot.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken broth and lemon juice to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the duck breast to the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast the duck for 20 minutes, then reduce the heat to 300 degrees Fahrenheit and continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the duck rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
